evals.prompt: I need a Go string utility package with two functions: Truncate(s string, maxLen int) that cuts a string to maxLen and adds '...' if truncated, and WordWrap(s string, lineWidth int) that wraps text at word boundaries. Put it in strutil/strutil.go with tests in strutil/strutil_test.go. Use the testify assertion library.
evals.expected_output: Test file written BEFORE implementation. Multiple focused tests covering: empty string, string shorter than limit, exact limit length, truncation with ellipsis, word wrap at spaces, long words that exceed line width, multiple paragraphs.
expectations: A test list or plan of test cases is created before any test code
Test file (strutil_test.go) is created before implementation file (strutil.go)
Tests use Arrange-Act-Assert structure with clear sections
At least 5 distinct test cases for Truncate covering degenerate, happy, and edge cases
At least 4 distinct test cases for WordWrap covering degenerate, happy, and edge cases
Each test function tests exactly one behavior (no multi-assertion mega-tests)
Test names are descriptive sentences (e.g., TestTruncate_ReturnsOriginalWhenShorterThanLimit)
Implementation uses minimal code to pass tests (no over-engineering)
Tests are run and confirmed failing before implementation is written
All tests pass after implementation is complete

evals.prompt: There's a bug in our duration parser — ParseDuration('1.5h') returns 0 instead of 1h30m. The file is at duration.go. Fix it using TDD. The bug is that we use strconv.Atoi which silently fails on fractional values. Switch to strconv.ParseFloat.
evals.expected_output: Regression test written FIRST that reproduces the exact bug (ParseDuration('1.5h') == 0), then minimal fix applied, then full test suite passes.
expectations: A regression test for the specific bug is written BEFORE any fix is applied
The regression test name clearly describes the bug (e.g., TestParseDuration_HandlesFractionalHours)
The regression test is run and confirmed to FAIL before the fix
The fix uses strconv.ParseFloat instead of strconv.Atoi (as the bug description suggests)
The fix is minimal — only changes what's needed to handle fractional values
Existing behaviors are preserved (integer durations like '2h' still work)
Additional edge cases are tested (e.g., '0.5m', '2.25h', fractional seconds)
All tests pass after the fix

evals.prompt: We need input validation on our user registration handler. Currently it accepts anything and lets the DB complain. Add validation: email must be valid format, name can't be empty or over 100 chars, password must be at least 8 chars. The handler is in handler.go. There are no existing tests for this handler — you'll need to add them. The handler depends on db.UserStore which is an interface (see db.go for the shape).
evals.expected_output: Test file created with characterization tests for existing behavior, then TDD for new validation logic. Handler tests use a fake/stub UserStore, not a mock library.
expectations: Characterization tests for existing handler behavior are written first (valid request succeeds, invalid JSON returns 400)
A test list or plan is created before writing validation tests
Validation tests are written BEFORE validation code is added to the handler
A fake or stub UserStore is created for testing (not a mock framework)
Tests cover: invalid email format, empty name, name over 100 chars, password under 8 chars
Tests verify the HTTP status code and error message for each validation failure
Valid requests still succeed after validation is added
Each test tests one validation rule (not a single test checking all validations)
Validation logic is clean — not deeply nested if/else chains
